assignment.name: ml_notebook
assignment.title: Train and evaluate a small image classifier on CIFAR-10
assignment.description: Produce a Jupyter notebook that loads CIFAR-10, trains an image classifier from scratch, and reports test accuracy with a confusion matrix and at least one ablation comparing two design choices.
constraints: Single notebook, runnable end-to-end on a free Colab T4 in under 30 minutes.
No pretrained weights; the model must be trained from scratch.
Final reported test accuracy must exceed 70%.
Include one ablation study (e.g. with vs. without data augmentation).
All hyperparameters declared in a single config cell at the top.

verdict.notes: The plan is feasible and well-scoped for Colab, but it's pitched at a lower conceptual bar than a systems modeling assignment. A 500k-parameter CNN on CIFAR-10 with standard augmentation is a fairly mechanical exercise—the ablation (augmentation on/off) is sensible but not particularly deep. To strengthen scope fit, the plan should add a second, more interesting ablation (e.g., architecture depth vs. width trade-off, or regularization strategy) that requires genuine reasoning about model design trade-offs rather than just flipping a boolean flag.

root.chosen_best_option: Small custom CNN (3-4 conv blocks with ~500k parameters)

branches.option: Small custom CNN (3-4 conv blocks with ~500k parameters)

history: Small custom CNN (3-4 conv blocks with ~500k parameters)

verdict.notes: The plan is feasible and the scope fits a 5-day window, but batch norm as the sole ablation is a missed opportunity for deeper insight. Data augmentation (on/off) would be more impactful and reveal more about generalization. The CNN architecture is sound and will likely hit 70%+ accuracy, but the conceptual framing is narrow—consider adding a second ablation (e.g., learning rate schedule or optimizer choice) to demonstrate stronger systems thinking about what drives classifier performance.

child.decision: Data augmentation strategy for ablation study

verdict.notes: ResNet-18 from scratch is the right complexity for the domain and 5 days is adequate for training + ablation on Colab. The plan is sound but risks underdelivering on *conceptual depth*: it's a straightforward baseline that doesn't demonstrate systems thinking or surprising model behavior. The ablation (augmentation vs. none) is valid but narrow; consider adding a second ablation on architecture choice (e.g., ResNet-18 vs. a smaller CNN) or regularization strategy to strengthen the comparative analysis and make the notebook more interesting pedagogically.

child.chosen_best_option: Standard augmentation (random crops, horizontal flips) vs. none

verdict.notes: The plan is feasible and the ablation is sound, but ResNet-18 from scratch on CIFAR-10 is a narrow, well-trodden path with limited conceptual novelty. Anand will ask: what systems thinking or modeling insight does training a standard architecture teach? The 70% accuracy threshold is reachable but tight on a T4 in 30 min—you're optimizing for a benchmark, not exploring. Consider instead comparing architectural *choices* (e.g. depth vs. width trade-offs, or skip connections vs. vanilla convolutions) to deepen the analysis beyond 'augmentation helps.'
